channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Nigerian priest in US dies by suicide after order to return home
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Nigerian Catholic priest in Massachusetts died by suicide after being ordered to return to Nigeria, where he feared for his life.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerian-priest-in-us-dies-by-suicide-after-order-to-return-home/

item.title: France move from Deschamps to Zidane wondering wha...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: France's Didier Deschamps era ended with a shock World Cup semifinal defeat to Spain. Is Zinedine Zidane better placed to get more out of this clearly talented group?

item.title: Maltese Cross lunges late to claim Grand Prix de Paris title
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Maltese Cross somehow defied bad luck in running to secure William Haggas’ first Grand Prix de Paris crown with a surging finish to complete a British one-two at ParisLongchamp.

item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: This article ranks the best-performing Nigerian commercial banks in 2025 based solely on their audited financial results. The post Best Nigerian commercial banks judging by their numbers in 2025 appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Nigerian govt gives deadline for final analogue TV switch-off
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Federal Government has fixed December 2028 as the deadline for final transition from analogue to digital television broadcasting. item.title: Nigerian Catholic priest dies after being told to leave US over expiring visa
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: A Nigerian Catholic priest, Rev. Benjamin Madu who had been preparing to leave the United States after his religious worker visa expired has died in Massachusetts, church officials have confirmed. item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has arrested a suspect and recovered N5 million allegedly stolen from a motorist shortly after he withdrew the money from a commercial bank in Bauchi. item.title: Youth leader killed in fresh Benue community attack
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A youth leader identified as Sunday Ogoh has been killed after suspected gunmen carried out another deadly attack on Ondo community in Ugboju District of Otukpo Local Government Area of Benue State. item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] Fresh issues identified in the proposed law require a comprehensive review to ensure the framework aligns with Nigeria's security architecture.

item.title: Provide accommodation, transport or lose corps members, NYSC warns employers
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) has warned employers of corps members to improve their welfare by providing accommodation and read more Provide accommodation, transport or lose corps members, NYSC warns employers

item.description: Across Nigeria’s 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory, these are the top five Nigerian news stories you shouldn’t miss. Sultan declares July 16 as the first day of Safar 1448AH The Sultan of Sokoto, Alhaji Muhammad Sa’ad Abubakar III, has announced that Thursday, July 16, 2026, will mar

item.description: The Court of Appeal, on Tuesday, reserved judgment in the appeals challenging the judgment that ordered the INEC's deregisteration of ADC, Accord Party and... The post Court reserves judgement in appeals against deregistration of ADC, Accord Party, three others appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Spain make history after beating France 2-0
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Spain on Tuesday made history after beating France 2-0 in the World Cup semi-final encounter. Two goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro earned Spain a deserved victory over France. The semi-final match was played at Dallas Stadium as Spain progressed to the final of the World Cup. Acc

item.title: Imo: Police foil suspected robbery operation, arrest one suspect
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Operatives of the Imo State Police Command have foiled a suspected armed robbery operation and arrested one suspect identified as Onyegbulem Lucky Boy, a native of Umunoha in Mbaitoli council area of Imo State. During a coordinated intelligence-led operation at Works Layout, Owerri, the operatives r

item.title: Nigeria: Rescued Oyo Pupils, Teachers Reunite With Families
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] Victims' reunions with their families came a day after the victims were formally handed over to the Oyo State Government by the Nigerian military.

item.title: Sultan declares Thursday first day of Safar 1448AH
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Sultan of Sokoto, His Eminence Muhammad Sa’ad Abubakar III, has declared Thursday, July 16, 2026, as the first day of Safar 1448AH after the crescent marking the beginning of the new Islamic month was not sighted across Nigeria. The declaration followed reports submitted by the National Mo

item.title: Ajaokuta Steel revival: Coalition rejects ‘empty promises’
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A powerful coalition of civil society organisations has dismissed recent investment tours and pledges for Nigeria’s long-dormant Ajaokuta Steel Company as insufficient, calling instead for an urgent, The post Ajaokuta Steel revival: Coalition rejects ‘empty promises’ appeared first on Tribune Online

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Gary Neville reacts to France’s defeat against Spain
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Manchester United defender Gary Neville said he was stunned after Spain defeated France 2-0 in the semi-final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Les Bleus came into the game as favourites, but will now settle for the third-place play-off. Mikel Oyazarbal’s penalty and Pedro Porro’s composed finish s

item.title: Nigeria, Ghana, Côte d’Ivoire move to end raw cocoa exports
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: "The story of the cocoa bean is, in miniature, the story of our continent—blessed beyond measure at the root and shortchanged at the branch... The post Nigeria, Ghana, Côte d’Ivoire move to end raw cocoa exports appeared first on Tribune Online.
